title: The stray
premise: A living thing, keeping its distance.

stages.narration: A dog, ribs like a xylophone, watching you from half a street off. Not sick — just starving, and still alive. Its tail moves once, uncertain, and stops.

choices.label: Set down a little food and step back
Crouch and wait, and let it decide
You can't feed yourself and a dog — go

notes: living-thing. Feeding spends a real ration for a big morale lift (offered only when you carry food); crouching is a smaller, free comfort; leaving is the pragmatic ache. Living animals persist — reinforces the human-only-undead canon.
